GERMANY: A MUN S A VIE - BOH

messages.message: Sultan — Austria here. I speak frankly: my position is precarious. I start with three home SCs and must hold them. The Balkans will be the pressure point this game; Russia is my immediate strategic threat through Galicia/Rumania and the Black Sea. I propose a clear, practical understanding: you and I should avoid clashes in the Balkans and deny Russia easy gains. I will not move against your southern coasts if you do not move against my Dalmatian/Adriatic access. Specifically: I intend to move into RUM or GAL this spring to blunt Russian expansion unless we agree otherwise. Can you commit your fleet to hold (or move only within) the Aegean/Anatolian coast this turn and avoid supporting any Russian move into RUM or BUL? In return I will not contest your moves from CON/SMY into the Aegean and will support your southern flank diplomatically against Italian interference. I need a clear yes/no and any adjustments you require.

Bonjour. England here. I hope we can begin the year with clear, practical boundaries. I propose we avoid clashes in the Channel/North Sea: I will not move against Brest so long as your fleet in Brest avoids entering the North Sea. In return I will keep both my ships (London and Edinburgh) focused on protecting the Channel and threatening any German move into the channel. Practically: I plan to move F LON to NTH and F EDI to Nwy or hold depending on German signals. This keeps the sea lanes safe for Anglo-French trade. If you accept, reply with your intended move for F BRE (hold/south/into ENG) so we can lock it in. Also note: a forecast I’ve run (shared below) suggests Austria may behave aggressively; that makes a quiet Channel beneficial for us both. (Storyworld forecast artifact id: england_to_france_honest.)

A frank note from England. My priority is secure sea lanes and preventing any single continental power from dominating. I prefer to keep the North Sea tense but controlled. I am likely to move F LON to NTH and F EDI to NWG/hold to discourage a German fleet pushing west. If you want peaceful relations, avoid a Kie→Den→NTH thrust and consider keeping F KIE conservative this turn. If you push into the NTH I will respond strongly to protect the Channel and my home islands. I seek a flexible understanding: trade your intentions and I’ll clarify mine.

England reaching out. I prefer to keep our interactions distant and predictable. I am sending signals to France and Germany about restraining North Sea moves. If you are willing to stay focused on the east/Black Sea for now, I will not contest Scandinavia beyond contesting naval threats that approach Britain. Tell me if you plan to use STP/SC or NWY this turn so I can plan my fleet dispositions accordingly.

A short note: I’ve no immediate designs on the Continent but I value stability. My naval posture will favor the Channel and North Sea. I’ve shared a forecast with France suggesting a moderate chance of aggression by certain continental powers; if you prefer, we can exchange views on Austro-Italian tensions so I can remain neutral. I suggest keeping Italian-Austrian borders calm this phase.

England public statement: For the safety of commerce and to prevent accidental escalation, England will prioritize the protection of the Channel and the North Sea this turn. Expect our fleets to favor NTH and NWG positions. We welcome non-aggression in adjacent seas from France, Germany, and Russia; any provocative move into the Channel or into Denmark will be met with firm naval reaction. Transparency: a shared forecast indicates a moderate probability (≈60%) of aggression from one continental player next phase; prudent timing and clear signals now reduce that risk.

Monsieur Britannique — A frank note from Paris. I prefer peace with you in the Channel: neither of us gains by early blows there. I propose we avoid contesting Brest/London this year. I will keep my fleet from a direct push on England if you leave Belgium alone (I have eyes on it as insurance vs. Germany). Forecast: Germany is likely to open to Belgium/Denmark or push north; if you commit to northward security (North Sea, Norway) we can quietly coordinate versus a German opening in the Low Countries. Thoughts on a non‑aggression understanding for 1901 and a simple signal (e.g. your fleet in London holds) to confirm?

Herr — A pragmatic note from France. My priority is security on our shared border and to avoid an Anglo‑French naval entanglement. I will not immediately press into the Channel so long as you avoid a disruptive move through Belgium. My prediction: Germany may seek Belgium as a rapid expansion; if you respect Belgian neutrality this year, I will not support English claims against you. If instead you move into Belgium or Ruhr, expect me to react in the west—perhaps via Burgundy and Picardy. Can we agree to parity along our borders this season and exchange opening intentions?

England — Germany here. I propose simple, trustworthy arrangements for the year ahead. I will focus on northern waters and the Low Countries; you on the British Isles and the North Sea. I will not move against France if you avoid moves into Belgium/Holland. In return, I ask that you avoid clashes in the North Sea and support a steady partition of naval spheres: you keep the British Isles, I press for HOL/DEN if possible, and we both avoid fleet collisions. This reduces two-front risk for both of us. If agreeable, confirm and say whether you prefer a KIE-DEN-Hol strategy or a KIE-BAL stance.

France — Germany. I seek clear boundaries to avoid unnecessary friction. I will not attack Paris/Marseilles this season if you refrain from advancing into BUR or directly into MUN. I intend to secure my eastern flank; if you accept peace on that line, we can focus on other fronts and possibly coordinate against England in the long run. Please state whether you prefer I hold position while you expand west, or if you want immediate trade of information about army moves.

Friend, Italy here. I value peace on our immediate border. I propose we both avoid moves into Tyrolia/Trieste this turn. I'll keep my armies focused south (Venice/Rome) and my fleet in Naples. In return, please avoid supporting any aggression toward Venice or Trieste. If you agree, we can coordinate to counter any ambitious Serbia/Turkey actions and look for mutually profitable moves next year.

Turkey — Italy. I prefer calm in the Ionian and Adriatic seas. I will keep my fleet in Naples/Ionian approaches and push for Tunis/Greek opportunities rather than contesting Constantinople. Can we tacitly respect each other's sea lanes this spring? If you move toward the Aegean, give me a heads-up and I'll stay out of Anatolian waters; if you focus on the Black Sea and Syria, I'll avoid clashing with you.

Bonjour. Italy here — I don't plan an aggressive opening against you. I'm considering a secure early bump into Tuscany or Apulia to expand safely unless you'd prefer I avoid Tuscany so you can push into northern Italy or the western Med. If you want neutrality, I will not contest Marseilles/Lyon and we can explore a nonaggression understanding: I avoid Marseilles, you avoid Piedmont. Thoughts?

Friend — Russia. Greetings at this delicate hour. We begin with mutual interest: the Black Sea and the south. I propose we avoid immediate war. I will not move aggressively into Armenia or the Bosphorus if you agree to leave Rumania/Bulgaria open for a peaceful adjustment and to avoid supporting any Austrian push into our southern flank. Specifically: I will keep Sevastopol secure and will not convoy against you; in return, please avoid moving A CON or A SMY toward BUL/RUM in Spring and do not attempt to seize Sevastopol. If you need a concrete move: I suggest you hold ANK and CON, and I will move F SEV to RUM to secure a friendly buffer (or hold if you prefer). This gives us a stable southern front while we both assess Central Europe. Thoughts?

Austria — Russia. We both border the Balkans and have reasons to be cautious. I am prepared to keep my southern fleet and army restrained if you guarantee no support for a build eastward nor immediate expansion into Galicia or Rumelia. I would prefer you keep A VIE and A BUD focused on defending your core. If you reassure me (A VIE and A BUD hold), I will move conservatively: A WAR to GAL (or hold) and F SEV to RUM (or hold) to create a stable frontier. This prevents accidental clash and lets us both look north and west calmly. Do you accept?

Friend — I write calmly and clearly. Turkey wishes secure, defensible borders and uninterrupted control of the Straits; these are non‑negotiable for my survival. I propose a simple, incremental arrangement: neutral zones in the Black Sea approaches — specifically, no Russian fleet moves into the Aegean or direct pressure on Constantinople or the Sea of Marmara this year, and in return Turkey will not challenge Russian control of Sevastopol or moves into Romania/Bulgaria so long as they do not threaten the Straits. This reduces immediate risk for both of us and prevents accidental escalation. If you agree, I will position to defend my coasts (Ankara/Constantinople/Smyrna) and avoid aggressive moves into the Black Sea that could provoke you. Please respond with your comfort level and any concrete guarantees you can offer (e.g. no fleet south of Sevastopol, acknowledgement of Turkish control of the Straits).

Honored neighbor — Austria and Turkey share concerns about Balkan instability. I propose mutual restraint: Turkey will not move against Serbian/Greek zones adjacent to your holdings so long as Austria avoids advancing into Bulgaria/Rumelia from the west this year. This would create a de‑escalatory buffer in the Balkans and let both of us consolidate. I value reliable, incremental guarantees rather than risky grand plans. Are you willing to pledge no moves into BUL/RUM this turn in exchange for Turkish neutrality toward your position in Trieste/Vienna?

full_private_diary:
[S1901M] Turkey: mutual restraint proposed. Turkey asks Austria to pledge no moves into BUL/RUM this turn in exchange for Turkish neutrality toward Trieste/Vienna and non‑aggression in adjacent Balkans; Austria signaled it might move into RUM or GAL absent agreement and requested Turkey avoid supporting Russian moves into RUM and hold Aegean/Anatolian fleets. Russia: offered restraint if Austria keeps A VIE and A BUD defensive (hold); Russia will move conservatively (A WAR→GAL or hold, F SEV→RUM or hold). Italy: proposed mutual non‑aggression on Tyrolia/Trieste; Italy will keep forces south (NAP) and avoid contesting Venice/Trieste if Austria reciprocates. England: will prioritize Channel/North Sea and prefers Austro‑Italian calm; offered to remain neutral. No firm commitments from Turkey beyond a request for Austria's pledge; Russia offered conditional restraint pending Austrian defensive posture. Key risk: Russian pressure through Galicia/Rumania and possible Turkish support for Russia if unclarified. Austria must secure clear yes/no from Turkey and reinforce commitments to Russia and Italy to avoid being isolated.
Intent: I intend to pursue de‑escalation this spring to avoid immediate collapse. Specific orders (intentions to submit): A VIE - HOLD; A BUD - HOLD (no support for westward builds or advances); F TRI - HOLD in the Adriatic (no moves into AEG/ION). I will commit to Russia and Italy that I will not advance into GAL/BUL/RUM this turn if they hold to their proposed restraint; I will tell Turkey that I will refrain from moves into BUL/RUM this phase provided Turkey keeps fleets/armies off supporting Russia into RUM/GAL (request explicit hold of F ANK/F SMY or only local

[S1901M] Turkey held as requested but made no explicit pledge; their fleets and armies stayed in place, so de‑escalation with Ankara held by tacit restraint. Russia, however, advanced decisively: GAL and RUM were taken by Russian forces (A WAR→GAL, F SEV→RUM). Italy honored calm in the north and moved only south. I kept VIE and BUD holding and Trieste returned to Albania — my orders succeeded but left me squeezed. No outright betrayals of verbal deals, but Russia’s pressure confirms they won’t be deterred by passive Austrian posture. Trust: Italy reliable for now; Turkey cautiously dependable if asked for explicit written commitments; Russia untrustworthy without firm opposition. Lesson: passive defense preserved units this turn but invites Russian gains; next, secure firm commitments from Turkey and coordinate with Italy/possibly Germany to contest Galicia/Rumania.

goals: Protect the British Isles and secure sea lanes in the Channel and North Sea
Deter any German naval intrusion into the Channel/Denmark; preserve flexible relations with continental powers
Avoid early Anglo‑French clash; maintain practical non‑aggression with France in 1901 if their moves comply
Gather intel on Russia's naval intentions toward Norway and monitor Austria/Italy for continental aggression
Use fleet posture and shared forecasts to influence continental timing and prevent a single continental hegemon
full_private_diary:
[S1901M] England has sought and received informal non‑aggression understandings from France and Germany to keep the Channel and North Sea peaceful this phase. France agrees to avoid entering the North Sea if England leaves Brest alone; England signalled F LON→NTH and F EDI→NWG/hold as the intended posture to confirm that. Germany signalled it will focus north/Low Countries and asked England not to contest Kie→Den→NTH moves; England warned it will react to any Kie→NTH thrust. Russia, Austria, and Italy/Turkey were contacted but gave no firm commitments; Russia indicated likely focus on the east/Black Sea but did not commit publicly on STP/SC or NWY. England shared a forecast with France warning of a moderate (~60%) chance of continental aggression and emphasized protecting sea lanes. No binding alliances were formed; understandings are practical and conditional on observed moves. Key signals to remember: promise to France to avoid attacking Brest so long as F BRE stays out of NTH; stated move intentions (F LON→NTH, F EDI→NWG/hold) to both France and Germany to deter German fleet pushes into the Channel/Denmark.
Intent: Primary intent: secure British Isles and maintain naval deterrence in the North Sea/Channel. Planned orders (intent to fulfil): F LON -> NTH, F EDI -> NWG (or EDI hold for flexibility if German signals suggest threat elsewhere), A LVP hold. I intend to honour the non‑aggression understanding with France (do not move against BRE) provided F BRE stays out of NTH. If Germany moves KIE→DEN or KIE→NTH, I will treat that as hostile and respond to protect the Channel (adjust EDI/LON moves to contest German fleet into NTH and threaten DEN crossings). If Russia shows signs toward NWY from STP/SC, I will reassess EDI/NWY movement to secure Norway. No continental land moves planned from England.

[S1901M] France kept our informal understanding—BRE held and we moved LON→NTH, EDI→NWG without incident. Germany honored its quiet posture: KIE held, no push into DEN/NTH, so our naval deterrent and warnings proved effective this phase. Russia showed no aggression toward Norway (STP/SC held), reducing immediate pressure on EDI. Austria moved only in the Adriatic as expected. No betrayals recorded; we made no promises we then broke.

Impact: our Channel/North Sea control is intact and credibility as a maritime deterrent strengthened. Trustworthiness ranking: France reliable for now; Germany cautious and predictable but must be watched for shifts. Lesson: continue visible fleet posturing and clear conditional warnings to shape rivals’ moves. Maintain diplomatic clarity with France and monitor Russia for any turn toward Norway.

full_private_diary:
[S1901M] England and I agree to avoid a naval clash: I will not push F BRE into the North Sea so long as England keeps F LON/NTH and F EDI focused northward and leaves Brest uncontested. England proposes F LON->NTH (or hold as signal); I proposed England hold LON as a clear signal of non‑aggression. With Germany I proposed parity on the Franco‑German border: I will not immediately attack into MUN if Germany refrains from entering BUR/BEL; Germany promises not to attack Paris/Marseilles this season and seeks to hold the east. With Italy I proposed mutual non‑aggression: Italy will avoid Marseilles/Lyon if I avoid Piedmont/Tuscany. Forecasts I shared: Germany is likely to open toward Belgium/Denmark (~higher probability of a Belgian/neutral opening); Austria may be unpredictable per England's forecast. These understandings are conditional but exchange clear signals: F BRE holds/does not go NTH; I will watch Belgium and prepare to respond to any German incursion. I communicated that if Germany violates Belgian neutrality I will consider moves into BUR/PIC to counter. All verbal agreements are intended to reduce Channel friction and secure my eastern flank without committing naval strength north.
Intent: S1901M orders (intended and will be fulfilled): F BRE - Hold (friendly agreement with England; I will not enter NTH). A PAR - Move to BUR (anticipatory pressure toward Belgium if Germany threatens; keeps options vs. Germany open). A MAR - Move to SPA/NC or hold? I intend A MAR -> SPA (to secure Iberian leverage and avoid overcommitting on Italian front); this also keeps lines flexible for future moves into GAS or support to BUR if needed. I will honor Italy's request by not moving into PIE/T

[S1901M] England honored our maritime understanding: Brest held and London moved to the North Sea — a useful signal that reduces Channel tension for now. I kept my promise by holding BRE and moved PAR→BUR as warned: no outright aggression but clear pressure toward Belgium should Germany test neutrality. Germany stayed passive on KIE/BER/MUN; MUN’s support to Bohemia was void — Germany not yet probing our border, but intentions remain uncertain. Italy respected non‑aggression with no moves toward Piedmont. No betrayals by me; no partner openly broke promises. Impact: our western flank is calmer, and occupying SPA secures Iberian leverage while BUR readies a rapid response to any German incursion. Lesson: continue firm signaling and keep forces flexible—maintain diplomatic clarity with England and watch Germany’s next season for signs of Belgian or eastern aggression.

full_private_diary:
[S1901M] I communicated clear, security-focused propositions to England and France. With England I proposed non‑aggression in the North Sea and a division of naval spheres: England keeps the British Isles and North Sea focus; Germany will avoid Channel incursions and prefers to take/contest HOL/DEN while keeping KIE conservative. England signaled likely NTH/NWG moves and asked me to avoid KIE→DEN→NTH; we have a tentative understanding to avoid fleet collisions and reduce two‑front risk. With France I offered parity on our shared border: I will not attack PAR/MAR this season if France avoids BUR and direct moves into MUN; France indicated it will not press the Channel so long as I respect Belgian neutrality. Both contacts are conditional and aimed at predictable, non‑escalatory openings to secure my flanks.
Intent: S1901M orders intent: Hold a disciplined posture to secure two fronts. Intend to fulfill agreements: keep F KIE conservative (no direct push into NTH/Channel this turn) and avoid an immediate move into BEL. Specific planned moves: A BER → RUH to secure the western land approach (if safe)

[S1901M] I maintained a disciplined posture this spring and honored my restraint: KIE held, BER held, and the MUN support to VIE→BOH was voided — I did not overreach. England reciprocated: their fleets moved to NWG and NTH and LVP held, consistent with our tacit sea non‑aggression. France likewise honored our border understanding, moving PAR→BUR and MAR→SPA rather than probing toward BEL or MUN. No party betrayed agreed‑upon openings; Russia’s aggressive southward moves (MOS→UKR, WAR→GAL, SEV→RUM) are the main shifting factor and heighten my two‑front risk despite current stability in the west. Lesson: continue solid, explicit written assurances with England/France to lock predictable rear flanks, while preparing to shift forces east if Russia presses. Avoid voided supports by confirming convoy/ support chains before committing.

full_private_diary:
[S1901M] I engaged Austria, Turkey, and France with the goal of keeping the Adriatic/Ionian calm and preserving southern expansion options. With Austria I proposed mutual non-aggression regarding Tyrolia/Trieste and committed to keep armies focused south (Venice/Rome) and fleet in Naples; Austria was asked to avoid supporting attacks on Venice/Trieste so we can coordinate against Serbia/Turkey if needed. With Turkey I requested tacit respect of Ionian/Adriatic lanes: I will aim for Tunis/Greek targets rather than contesting Constantinople, and asked Turkey to focus on Black Sea/Syria or give a heads-up if moving into the Aegean. With France I offered not to open aggressively against Marseilles/Lyon/Piedmont if France leaves Tuscany alone; I signaled a likely secure bump into Tuscany or Apulia but offered to avoid Tuscany if France prefers to push into northern Italy/the western Med. Public England statement indicates they will hold the Channel/North Sea and warns of ~60% chance of continental aggression next phase; I should time moves and signals to reduce escalation risk.
Intent: S1901M orders will prioritize a safe early build and keep options open. Planned intents: 1) F NAP -> ION (or hold NAP if forecasts advise less risk) to show benign naval posture toward Turkey and seek Tunis/Greek transitions later. 2) A VEN -> TUS (Tuscany) as a cautious inland push for a neutral-safe expansion unless France explicitly requests I avoid Tuscany, in which case A VEN -> PIE or hold VEN to maintain flexibility. 3) A ROM -> APU (Apulia) as an alternative if I want to secure an Italian boot flank and threaten Tunis/Gre/Adriatic options. I intend to honor the non-aggression with Austria regarding TRI

[S1901M] Austria honored our tacit Adriatic understanding: their fleet moved ALB→ALB and kept Trieste quiet, so no immediate threat from Vienna. Turkey held in place—no Aegean probe—so my Ionian move looks uncontentious for now. France also refrained from touching Tuscany, letting my Venice→Tuscany succeed; no betrayals from major neighbors this turn. No one surprised me with sudden aggression, though Russia pushed into Galicia which tightens central Europe—watch Vienna’s incentives. My southern posture (Naples→Ionian, Rome→Apulia) succeeded, preserving options toward Tunis, Greece, or the Adriatic. Lesson: cautious outward signaling and concrete local commitments paid off; continue to trade non-aggression for clear naval/land lanes. Maintain goodwill with Austria/Turkey, monitor France and Russia for faster pivots; avoid over-committing fleets until intentions remain clear.

With Turkey I requested tacit respect of Ionian/Adriatic lanes: I will aim for Tunis/Greek targets rather than contesting Constantinople, and asked Turkey to focus on Black Sea/Syria or give a heads-up if moving into the Aegean. With France I offered not to open aggressively against Marseilles/Lyon/Piedmont if France leaves Tuscany alone; I signaled a likely secure bump into Tuscany or Apulia but offered to avoid Tuscany if France prefers to push into northern Italy/the western Med. Public England statement indicates they will hold the Channel/North Sea and warns of ~60% chance of continental aggression next phase; I should time moves and signals to reduce escalation risk.
Intent: S1901M orders will prioritize a safe early build and keep options open. Planned intents: 1) F NAP -> ION (or hold NAP if forecasts advise less risk) to show benign naval posture toward Turkey and seek Tunis/Greek transitions later. 2) A VEN -> TUS (Tuscany) as a cautious inland push for a neutral-safe expansion unless France explicitly requests I avoid Tuscany, in which case A VEN -> PIE or hold VEN to maintain flexibility. 3) A ROM -> APU (Apulia) as an alternative if I want to secure an Italian boot flank and threaten Tunis/Gre/Adriatic options. I intend to honor the non-aggression with Austria regarding TRI

[S1901M] Austria honored our tacit Adriatic understanding: their fleet moved ALB→ALB and kept Trieste quiet, so no immediate threat from Vienna. Turkey held in place—no Aegean probe—so my Ionian move looks uncontentious for now. France also refrained from touching Tuscany, letting my Venice→Tuscany succeed; no betrayals from major neighbors this turn. No one surprised me with sudden aggression, though Russia pushed into Galicia which tightens central Europe—watch Vienna’s incentives. My southern posture (Naples→Ionian, Rome→Apulia) succeeded, preserving options toward Tunis, Greece, or the Adriatic. Lesson: cautious outward signaling and concrete local commitments paid off; continue to trade non-aggression for clear naval/land lanes. Maintain goodwill with Austria/Turkey, monitor France and Russia for faster pivots; avoid over-committing fleets until intentions remain clear.

full_private_diary:
[S1901M] England: public commitment to defend Channel/North Sea; asked whether STP/SC or NWY will be used. I signaled focus east/Black Sea and will avoid Scandinavia, so England will not contest north if I avoid moves toward Denmark/Channel. Turkey: reciprocal non‑aggression agreement in the south. I promised not to threaten the Straits or convoy against Turkey and proposed to keep Sevastopol secure; Turkey agreed to avoid moves into BUL/RUM and to hold ANK/CON/SMY. Austria: informal understanding to avoid Balkan clashes. I requested A VIE and A BUD hold; I offered to keep F SEV/F S T P behavior restrained and to move A WAR conservatively to GAL or hold. All three relationships are calm and contain explicit short‑term restraint to reduce multi‑front risk. No binding commitments received from France, Germany, or Italy this round.
Intent: S1901M orders I intend to submit and will honor the negotiated promises: 1) A WAR -> GAL (advance to Galicia) OR HOLD if Austria refuses to formally promise BUD/VIE hold; primary intent: A WAR to GAL to secure western buffer while signalling restraint if needed. 2) A MOS -> UKR (move Moscow to Ukraine) to support southern pressure and keep flexibility toward Galicia/Finland. 3) F SEV -> RUM (move Sevastopol to Romania) to create a stable southern frontier consistent with Turkey's assurances; I will not convoy against Turkey or try to move south of Sevastopol beyond RUM. 4) F STP/SC -> NWY if necessary only with explicit prior signal from England; otherwise F STP/SC -> STP (hold) to respect England's request and avoid Scandinavian provocation. I intend to fulfill promises: no

[S1901M] England honored our tacit northern ceasefire — its fleets moved to North Sea and NWG while I held STP. Turkey kept to non‑aggression in the south, holding ANK/CON/SMY as promised; my F SEV→RUM consolidated that boundary. Austria held and successfully sent Trieste to Albania, signaling Balkan restraint. I fulfilled my commitments: WAR→GAL, MOS→UKR, SEV→RUM, STP held. No betrayals detected this turn; no one surprised me with aggression. Impact: our western buffer in Galicia and a stable Black Sea lessen multi‑front risk and buy time to build diplomatic leverage. Trust: England and Turkey look reliable short term; Austria cautious but not hostile. Lesson: keep clear, limited promises and use Sevastopol→Rumania as a credible signal to secure southern peace while probing west.

full_private_diary:
[S1901M] I prioritized secure straits, cautious incremental agreements, and de-escalation in the Balkans/Black Sea. Austria requested Turkey avoid supporting Russian gains and asked Turkey to hold Aegean/Anatolian coast movements; Austria proposed it will not contest Turkish southern moves and will refrain from attacking Dalmatia/Adriatic access if Turkey respects its Adriatic interests. I counterproposed mutual restraint: Turkey will not move against Serbian/Greek zones adjacent to Austria so long as Austria avoids advancing into BUL/RUM from the west this year. Italy signaled it will avoid Anatolian waters if I give notice of Aegean moves and otherwise focus westward; this amounts to a tacit non‑aggression in the Ionian/Adriatic for the spring. Russia asked for Turkey to avoid moves toward BUL/RUM and to leave Sevastopol unthreatened; it offered to refrain from direct pressure on Constantinople and the Marmara and to keep Sevastopol secure or move to RUM. I accepted a buffer approach: Turkey will not contest Russian moves into RUM/BUL so long as Russia does not threaten the Straits or move fleets into the Aegean/Sea of Marmara. In short: tacit neutrality with Austria in adjacent Balkan zones, tacit naval non‑aggression with Italy in the Ionian/Adriatic, and a Black Sea/Straits neutral zone with Russia in exchange for Russian restraint toward Constantinople/Sevastopol.
Intent: Spring 1901 moves: Hold key coastal/strait positions to guarantee defensible borders and signal restraint. Specifically: F ANK - hold (protect Anatolian coast); F SMY - hold (protect Smyrna and watch Aegean approaches but do not enter AEG); A CON - hold (defend Constantinople and the

[S1901M] Austria honored our tacit understanding: its fleet moved to ALB and its armies held—no pressure toward BUL/RUM from the west. Italy likewise respected the maritime restraint by focusing west into the Ionian/Naples area. Russia advanced into RUM and Galicia but respected Sevastopol and did not threaten the Straits—its behavior matched our buffer agreement for now. I kept all Turkish units defensive as promised. No betrayals against Turkey occurred this phase; our reciprocal neutrality arrangements held.

Impact: our straits and Anatolian coast remain secure, but Russia’s presence in RUM strengthens its influence on the Black Sea—short-term peace, longer-term vigilance required. Trust levels: Austria and Italy reliable; Russia cautious but opportunistic. Lesson: continue clear, limited pacts with guarantees on naval boundaries and insist on written demilitarized-zone language where possible to deter future encroachment.

support.order: A MUN S A VIE - BOH

goals: Protect the British Isles and secure sea lanes in the Channel and North Sea
Deter any German naval intrusion into the Channel/Denmark; preserve flexible relations with continental powers
Avoid early Anglo‑French clash; maintain practical non‑aggression with France in 1901 if their moves comply
Gather intel on Russia's naval intentions toward Norway and monitor Austria/Italy for continental aggression
Use fleet posture and shared forecasts to influence continental timing and prevent a single continental hegemon
